heading: Violations by lessee
label: 8737 Violations by lessee
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7437(11th par., less proviso).34 U.S.C. 524(11th par., less proviso, of amended 3d and 4th provisos), ; , ; , .June 4, 1920, ch. 22841 Stat. 813June 30, 1938, ch. 851, § 152 Stat. 1254June 17, 1944, ch. 26258 Stat. 281In subsection (a) the words “the district in which any part of the property is located” are substituted for the words “the district in which the property, or some part thereof, is located,” for brevity.In subsection (b) the words “for resort to” and “for” are omitted as surplusage.

children.label: a
children.text: If a lessee fails to comply with any provision of this chapter, of his lease, or of regulations issued under that are in force on the date of his lease, the lease may be forfeited and cancelled by an appropriate proceeding in the United States district court for the district in which any part of the property is located.section 8736 of this title

children.label: b
children.text: The lease may provide appropriate methods for the settlement of disputes and remedies for breach of specified conditions.
